nodes.context: Collective of criminal groups originating from Corsica, operating in France, North Africa, and Latin America.
Italian organized crime group from Campania.
Italian organized crime group from Calabria.
Italian organized crime group from Sicily.

nodes.standard_name: Gang de la Brise de Mer
nodes.original_text_name: Gang de la Brise de Mer
nodes.context: Corsican criminal group, one of the largest in Corsica and France.
Corsican criminal group based in Ajaccio.
Corsican criminal group that sought to unify various groups.
Corsican criminal group considered successors of Brise de Mer.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: American Mafia , Camorra , Lebanese mafia , Milieu , 'Ndrangheta , Sicilian Mafia
edges.context: The Corsican mafia is allied with the American Mafia.

edges.target: Gang de la Brise de Mer
edges.evidence_quote: the Brise de Mer gang was born, which would subsequently become one of the largest criminal groups in Corsica and France.
edges.context: The Brise de Mer gang is a part of the Corsican mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: The Petit Bar Gang of Ajaccio and the Corsican mob of Marseille are also active.
edges.context: The Petit Bar Gang is a known group within the Corsican mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: one linked to the Petit Bar Gang in Ajaccio and another associated with the Mattei clan , which has sought to unify various groups.
edges.context: The Mattei clan is a group within the Corsican mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Known groups in the Corsican mafia are the Venzolasca Gang (nickname in reference to the village of Venzolasca , in northern Corsica, which are from key members of the gang), considered the Brise de Mer successors.
edges.context: The Venzolasca Gang is a known group within the Corsican mafia.

edges.source: Gang de la Brise de Mer
edges.evidence_quote: Known groups in the Corsican mafia are the Venzolasca Gang ... considered the Brise de Mer successors.
edges.context: The Venzolasca Gang is considered the successor of the Brise de Mer gang.
